key_insight: Quadrature accuracy in FEM is not a minor implementation detail: to preserve optimal convergence for mixed-boundary elliptic problems, volume and face integrals must meet specific precision thresholds tied to polynomial order p.
engineering_relevance: For higher-order FEM solvers, this gives a practical rule for choosing integration schemes that avoids hidden accuracy loss and helps ensure reliable error control in mixed Dirichlet-Robin settings.
method: Analyzes p-th order FEM for elliptic elliptic problems with mixed boundary conditions.
Derives required algebraic precision for volume quadrature (≥ 2p-2 for H1, ≥ max{p,2p-2} for L2).
Derives required algebraic precision for face quadrature (≥ 2p-1) and checks sharpness with linear-element examples.
evidence_signal: The paper supports its theory with numerical experiments and two sharpness examples showing that reduced quadrature can fail to preserve optimal L2 convergence for p=1.
watch_item: Using quadrature below the stated precision thresholds can degrade convergence, especially on boundary face terms, so implementation must match the FEM order and boundary condition type.
paper_title: The effect of numerical integration in the FEM for elliptic problems with mixed boundary conditions
